pattern: Insert each original number and its digit-reversed form into a set; the distinct count is the set's final size.
hints: The order of the final array is irrelevant; only which distinct values appear matters.
For each number add both it and its reversal to a set, where reversal drops any leading zeros naturally as an integer.
Reverse a number by repeatedly taking its last digit, then count the size of the set holding all originals and reversals.

edge_inputs.description: all identical collapse to one distinct
single element with a distinct reversal
trailing zero reversal loses a digit
number and its reverse already both present

stress.description: long random array across the full value range
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = rng.randint(1, max(1, size))
    nums = [rng.randint(1, 1000000) for _ in range(n)]
    return [nums]
stress.note: varied magnitudes produce many trailing-zero and collision cases
